frameworks.name: cognitive map
frameworks.one_line: hippocampal place cells implement a spatial map supporting flexible navigation

frameworks.name: place cell coding
frameworks.one_line: CA1 neurons with location-specific firing represent allocentric position

frameworks.name: environmental connectivity representation
frameworks.one_line: hypothesis that the map should encode available transitions between subspaces

core_question: Do hippocampal CA1 place cells encode environmental connectivity between subspaces in addition to spatial location?
key_assumptions: flexible navigation relies on a hippocampal cognitive map
optimal navigation requires tracking both location and available inter-subspace connections
place-cell firing fields can reveal what the map explicitly represents
manipulations: connectivity of saloon-type doors between four geometrically identical boxes (locked one or both directions)
repetitive geometry across four arranged boxes to probe global vs local coding
key_findings: rats behaviourally tracked changes in environmental connectivity
CA1 place cells did not respond to connectivity changes
place cells did not represent doorways differently from other locations
place cells coded space in a global frame with minimal repetitive fields
interpretations: CA1 provides a spatial map that does not explicitly include environmental connectivity
the hippocampal map prioritises global allocentric location over transition structure
